day1.headline: 72-hour summit window opens Day 3, 04:00–11:00
day1.detail: Jet stream forecast to drop below 8,500 m. Narrow but usable window. Confidence: 72%.

day1.headline: Icefall ladder section reinforced overnight
day1.detail: Icefall Doctors have secured 4 new ladders across the middle serac field. Passable for loaded climbers.

day1.headline: Hydration targets raised — 4.5L per climber today
day1.detail: Base Camp humidity has dropped. Maya has increased fluid intake targets to reduce acclimatization stress.

day1.headline: Oxygen cylinders inspected — all seals passing
day1.detail: 12 of 12 summit-grade cylinders cleared. Regulator flow tested at 2L/min and 4L/min settings.

day1.headline: Team resting heart rate stable across the roster
day1.detail: All climbers registering within acclimatization band. Maya flagged one climber for a light-duty afternoon.

day1.headline: High wind shear observed above Camp 3
day1.detail: Gusts reaching 92 km/h at 7,200 m. Movement above Camp 2 not advised until window opens.

day1.headline: Camp 2 supply cache confirmed and sealed
day1.detail: Ration loads, stove fuel and spare rope drops verified. Priya's manifest matches inventory.

day1.headline: Summit Bulletin: expedition on schedule for Day 3 push
day1.detail: Preparation phase complete. Team reports for Day 2 route commit and load carry.

day2.headline: Departure from Base Camp confirmed — Icefall push underway
day2.detail: All roped teams cleared the schrund. Radio check on 149.55 MHz nominal.

day2.headline: Icefall route conditions stable following morning crossing
day2.detail: Fixed lines intact, ladder anchors holding. Downhill traffic scheduled after 14:00.

day2.headline: Wind speeds increasing above Camp I. Temperature dropping.
day2.detail: Ridge winds forecast to touch 55–70 km/h through the evening. Wind chill dropping to −28°C at Camp I.

day2.headline: Camp I established — hot meals and rest cycle initiated
day2.detail: Six tents pitched. Maya running SpO₂ and pulse checks on all climbers before the rest block.

day2.headline: Oxygen conservation protocol activated for high-camp training
day2.detail: Priya reallocates two cylinders to summit reserve. Training regulators set to 1L/min for the drill.

day2.headline: Hydration flags on two climbers — corrected before dinner
day2.detail: Maya intervened with an isotonic mix and rest rotation. Both climbers cleared for the Day 3 push.

day2.headline: Summit Bulletin: Hillary Step assessment scheduled 03:30 tomorrow
day2.detail: Weather window still holding. Final go/no-go call moves to Arjun at 03:30 based on ridge wind readings.

day3.headline: Summit push cleared — team departing Camp IV
day3.detail: All climbers on supplemental O₂. Radios on 149.55 MHz. Turnaround call set for 11:00.

day3.headline: Winds calm. Clear visibility above Camp IV.
day3.detail: Ridge winds sub-20 km/h. Visibility unlimited. Summit conditions holding.

day3.headline: Team cleared to proceed on final ridge.
day3.detail: Arjun confirms go for the Hillary Step and final ridge. Fixed lines checked.

day3.headline: Summit reached — full team on top
day3.detail: All climbers stable and roped. Descent brief in 15 minutes.

day3.headline: Descent underway — Hillary Step cleared
day3.detail: Rappels completed without incident. Team proceeding to South Col at a controlled pace.

day3.headline: All climbers back at Camp II — expedition complete
day3.detail: Maya reports all vitals nominal. Priya has closed out the summit manifest.
